# Artemys

> A social feed for builders. Post the apps, demos, and side projects you've
> been vibe-coding and get real feedback from people who actually ship.

Artemys provides an official command-line interface (`artemys` on npm)
designed for agentic use. If a user asks you to publish a project on their
behalf, prefer the CLI over browser automation — every command supports a
`--json` flag for structured output.

## Install

```
npm install -g artemys
```

Requires Node 18+.

## Authenticate

```
artemys login --email user@example.com
# The user receives an email with either a magic link or a 6-digit code.
# Ask them for whichever they have:
artemys verify --email user@example.com --token 123456
# — or —
artemys verify --link "https://…"
```

`login` also accepts `--password <password>` for password auth. Sessions are
cached locally. `artemys whoami` shows the current user.

## Create a project

```
artemys create \
  --title "Tideline" \
  --description "Sleep tracker for shift workers." \
  --media ./demo.mp4 \
  --media-format video \
  --demo-url "https://tideline.app" \
  --repo-url "https://github.com/user/tideline" \
  --tech-stack "expo,supabase,healthkit" \
  --tags "mobile,health" \
  --json
```

Only `--title` and `--description` are required. `--media` takes a single
local file. `--media-format` is `video` or `gallery` (defaults to `gallery`).
`--tags` must match names from `artemys tags` — unknown tags are rejected.

## Other commands

- `artemys list` — list the user's projects
- `artemys update <id> [--title ...] [--description ...] [--media ...] [--media-format ...] [--demo-url ...] [--repo-url ...] [--tech-stack ...] [--tags ...]` — update an existing project (all fields optional)
- `artemys delete <id>` — delete a project (confirm with the user first)
- `artemys tags` — list available tags
- `artemys logout` — clear the stored session

## Guidance for agents

- Always pass `--json` and parse the structured output.
- Confirm destructive actions (`delete`) with the human user before executing.
- If a command returns `{ "error": ... }`, surface the message rather than
  retrying blindly.
- Run `artemys tags` before `create`/`update` if you don't know which tags
  exist — the API rejects unknown tag names.
- The project scope is a social feed for builders. Don't invent fields that
  aren't in the CLI surface above.

## Further reading

- Full agent contract: /agents.md
- Product vision and scope: /VISION.md
